_source: Cisco CCNA/CCNP Official Cert Guides, Network Warrior, TCP/IP Illustrated
_note: Long-tail network engineering. Routing protocols, VLANs, subnetting, troubleshooting, network design.

common_masks./31: 255.255.255.254 (2 addresses, RFC 3021; point-to-point only, no network/broadcast)

calculation_formula.example: /26 borrows 2 bits from /24; 2^2 = 4 subnets of 64 addresses (62 usable) each

vlsm_example.scenario: 192.168.1.0/24 network; need 100-host subnet, two 50-host subnets, three 10-host subnets

vlan_basics.purpose: Logical segmentation; broadcast domain separation; security; QoS
vlan_basics.default_vlan: VLAN 1; management VLAN by default; cannot be deleted
vlan_basics.voice_vlan: Separate VLAN for VoIP; QoS priority; typically VLAN 100 or similar

trunk_vs_access.access_port: Single VLAN; end device connection; untagged traffic
trunk_vs_access.trunk_port: Multiple VLANs; switch-to-switch or switch-to-router; tagged traffic (802.1Q)
trunk_vs_access.native_vlan: Untagged traffic on trunk; default VLAN 1; should match on both sides; security: change from VLAN 1

inter_vlan_routing.router_on_a_stick: Single router interface; subinterfaces for each VLAN; 802.1Q encapsulation
inter_vlan_routing.l3_switch_svi: Switch Virtual Interface; VLAN interface on L3 switch; 'ip routing' enabled; most common modern approach

modes.server: Create/delete/modify VLANs; propagate changes; default mode
modes.client: Cannot create VLANs; receive updates; typically access switches
modes.transparent: Can create local VLANs; forwards VTP advertisements; doesn't synchronize
modes.off: VTP disabled; doesn't forward advertisements; VTPv3 only

vtp.best_practice: Use transparent or off; VTP can accidentally delete all VLANs (higher revision number wins)
vtp.vtp_pruning: Prevents unnecessary flooding of traffic to switches without members in that VLAN

spanning_tree_protocol.purpose: Prevent Layer 2 loops; redundant links but only one active path at a time

stp_types.802_1d: Original STP; 50 second convergence (20 max age + 2×15 forward delay); slow
stp_types.rstp_802_1w: Rapid STP; <2 second convergence; backwards compatible; most common
stp_types.pvst_plus: Per-VLAN STP; Cisco proprietary; separate instance per VLAN; load balancing possible
stp_types.mstp_802_1s: Multiple STP; groups VLANs into instances; scales better than PVST+

port_states_rstp.discarding: Blocking; receives BPDUs only; doesn't forward
port_states_rstp.transition: Discarding → Learning → Forwarding (under 3 seconds with RSTP)

port_roles.root_port: Best path to root bridge; one per non-root switch
port_roles.designated_port: Forwarding port on segment; sends BPDUs
port_roles.alternate_port: Backup path to root; replaces root port if fails
port_roles.backup_port: Redundant designated port on same segment

root_bridge_election.criteria: Lowest bridge ID (priority + MAC); priority default 32768
root_bridge_election.best_practice: Manually set root; primary priority 24576, secondary 28672
root_bridge_election.command: spanning-tree vlan 10 root primary (sets priority to 24576 or 4096 less than current root)

portfast.purpose: Bypass listening/learning on access ports; immediate forwarding
portfast.when: End devices only (PCs, printers); never on trunk or ports to switches
portfast.bpduguard: Shutdown port if BPDU received; prevents loops from rogue switches

ospf.type: Link-state; open standard; hierarchical with areas; fast convergence
ospf.metric: Cost = 100,000,000 / bandwidth (bps); Gi = 1, 10Gi = 1 (need to adjust reference)
ospf.areas: Backbone Area 0 required; all other areas connect to Area 0; reduces LSAs and routing table size

dr_bdr_election.purpose: Reduce adjacencies on multi-access networks (Ethernet); all routers adjacent to DR/BDR only
dr_bdr_election.election: Highest priority (default 1); tie-break on highest router ID
dr_bdr_election.priority_0: Never becomes DR/BDR; use on spokes in hub-and-spoke
dr_bdr_election.preemption: No preemption; DR remains until failure even if higher priority router added

lsa_types.type_1: Router LSA; all routers in area; router links
lsa_types.type_2: Network LSA; DR on multi-access segments
lsa_types.type_3: Summary LSA; ABR; routes between areas

eigrp.type: Advanced distance-vector; Cisco proprietary; fast convergence; unequal cost load balancing
eigrp.metric: Composite: bandwidth (min), delay (sum), reliability, load; default uses bandwidth + delay only
eigrp.feasible_successor: Backup route; AD < FD of successor; immediate failover without query
eigrp.stuck_in_active: Router doesn't receive query replies; usually topology problem; check for network loops or asymmetric routing
eigrp.k_values: K1 (BW) = 1, K3 (delay) = 1, K2/K4/K5 = 0; must match for neighbor adjacency

bgp.type: Path-vector; EGP; internet routing; policy-based; slow convergence

ibgp_vs_ebgp.ibgp: Within same AS; full mesh or route reflectors; AD 200; doesn't change next-hop
ibgp_vs_ebgp.ebgp: Between different AS; AD 20; changes next-hop to self; TTL 1 (directly connected)

order: Weight (Cisco only; highest wins; local to router)

bgp.route_summarization: Reduces table size; aggregate-address command; can suppress more-specifics

osi_model_approach.layer_1: Physical; check cables, link lights, interface status (show interface); errors, CRC errors indicate bad cable/duplex
osi_model_approach.layer_2: Data Link; check MAC table, ARP, VLAN assignment, trunk; 'show mac address-table', 'show arp'
osi_model_approach.layer_3: Network; check routing table, default gateway, IP addressing; 'show ip route', ping, traceroute
osi_model_approach.layer_4_7: Transport+; check ACLs, NAT, firewall rules; 'show access-lists', 'show ip nat translations', telnet to port

duplex_mismatch.symptoms: Slow performance, CRC errors, late collisions
duplex_mismatch.detection: show interface - one side full, other half; or both auto but negotiated different
duplex_mismatch.fix: Hard-set both sides to full duplex or both to auto; never mix

vlan_mismatch.symptoms: No connectivity; same subnet but can't ping
vlan_mismatch.detection: show vlan brief; verify access port in correct VLAN

routing_loop.symptoms: Packets loop, TTL expires, high CPU, routing table flapping
routing_loop.detection: traceroute shows repeated hops; show ip route shows route flapping
routing_loop.fix: Correct metrics, add route filters, implement split horizon/poison reverse

asymmetric_routing.symptoms: Traffic works one way but not return; stateful firewall drops return
asymmetric_routing.detection: Packet capture on both sides; see outbound but no inbound reply
asymmetric_routing.fix: Ensure both directions use same path or firewall allows asymmetric flows

useful_commands.show_ip_interface_brief: Quick status of all interfaces; IP, method, status, protocol
useful_commands.show_ip_route: Routing table; codes (C=connected, S=static, O=OSPF, D=EIGRP, B=BGP)
useful_commands.show_cdp_neighbors: Cisco Discovery Protocol; see directly connected Cisco devices
useful_commands.show_spanning_tree: STP status, root bridge, port roles/states
useful_commands.show_ip_ospf_neighbor: OSPF adjacencies; state should be FULL or 2WAY
useful_commands.show_interfaces_status: Port status, VLAN, duplex, speed; err-disabled
useful_commands.debug_ip_packet: Real-time packet flow; use ACL to limit; 'debug ip packet <acl>'; CAUTION: high CPU

three_tier_hierarchy.core_layer: High-speed backbone; redundancy; no packet manipulation; L3 switching
three_tier_hierarchy.distribution_layer: Routing between VLANs; policy enforcement; aggregation point; L3 switches
three_tier_hierarchy.access_layer: End user connection; port security; QoS; PoE; L2 switches
three_tier_hierarchy.collapsed_core: Combine core and distribution for small networks; cost savings

redundancy.hsrp_vrrp: First Hop Redundancy Protocol; virtual IP/MAC; active/standby gateways; HSRP Cisco, VRRP standard
redundancy.etherchannel: Bundle multiple links as one logical link; LACP (802.3ad standard) or PAgP (Cisco); load balances traffic
redundancy.dual_homing: Servers connected to two switches; NIC teaming; active/active or active/passive

qos.classification: Mark packets (DSCP, CoS); trust or rewrite; at network edge
qos.queuing: Priority queue for voice (LLQ); WFQ or CBWFQ for data
qos.policing_shaping: Police drops excess; shaping buffers and sends; shaping preferred for TCP
qos.voice_requirements: Latency <150ms, jitter <30ms, loss <1%; EF (DSCP 46) marking; priority queue
